title: Motor Control Error
shortDescription: The motor control unit has detected an issue with the drive motor or its circuitry.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the motor inverter or control module cannot properly drive the washer's main motor. This can be due to a failed motor, a faulty inverter board, or wiring issues between the motor and control system. The washer will stop and will not agitate or spin.

causes.cause: Motor brushes are worn out (on brush-type motors)
Motor inverter board has failed
Motor winding has an open or short circuit
Wiring harness between motor and control board is damaged

symptoms: Drum does not spin or agitate
Motor hums but drum does not move

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then plug it back in. Run a test cycle. If E02 returns, proceed with further diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Check motor brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor from the rear panel. If the motor has carbon brushes, inspect them for wear. Brushes shorter than 1/4 inch need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and measure resistance across each winding pair. Typical readings are 2-10 ohms. Also check for ground faults between windings and the motor housing.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Check all wiring between the motor and the control module for damage, loose connections, or corrosion. Repair or replace as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or inverter board needs replacement, professional installation ensures proper calibration and testing.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E02 motor error fix

faq.q: What are motor brushes?
faq.a: Carbon brushes are small blocks that make electrical contact with the spinning commutator inside the motor. They wear down over time and need replacement.

faq.q: How long do Bosch washer motor brushes last?
faq.a: Typically 5-8 years with normal use. Heavy use or frequent high-spin cycles can accelerate wear.

faq.q: Can I still use the washer with E02?
faq.a: No. E02 prevents the drum from turning. The washer will not agitate or spin until the motor issue is resolved.

title: Motor Tachometer Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor (tachometer) is not providing a valid signal to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates that the tachometer (motor speed sensor) mounted on the motor is not sending expected speed feedback to the control board. The control board uses this signal to regulate drum speed during wash and spin cycles. Without it, the washer cannot maintain safe operation.

causes.cause: Tachometer sensor has failed
Tachometer magnet on the motor shaft is loose or missing
Wiring from tachometer to control board is damaged
Motor is overloaded and not reaching expected speed

symptoms: Drum spins erratically or not at all
Washer will not enter spin cycle
Unusual motor speed variations during wash
Cycle takes much longer than expected

fixSteps.title: Check for overload
fixSteps.instruction: An overloaded drum can prevent the motor from reaching target speed. Reduce the load size and try again. Bosch front-loaders should not be packed tightly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor from the rear panel. The tachometer is a small sensor attached to the motor housing or built into the motor assembly. Check that the sensor is firmly mounted and the magnet on the motor shaft is intact.

fixSteps.title: Test the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the tachometer wires and measure resistance. A typical reading is 100-300 ohms. Also spin the motor shaft by hand while checking for AC voltage output from the tachometer (should produce a small voltage).

fixSteps.title: Replace the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: If the tachometer tests bad, replace it. Remove the mounting screw or clip, disconnect the wiring, and install the new sensor. Ensure proper alignment with the motor shaft magnet.

whenToCallPro: If the tachometer and motor test fine, the control board may have a faulty input circuit requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E04 tachometer speed sensor error fix

faq.q: What is a tachometer in a washing machine?
faq.a: A tachometer is a small sensor that generates an electrical signal proportional to motor speed. The control board uses this signal to precisely control drum speed.

faq.q: Can overloading cause E04?
faq.a: Yes. An overloaded drum puts excessive strain on the motor, preventing it from reaching target speed. The tachometer reports the low speed, triggering E04.

faq.q: Is the tachometer the same as the Hall sensor?
faq.a: In some Bosch models, the tachometer uses a Hall effect sensor. Functionally they serve the same purpose of measuring motor speed.

title: Drain Timeout Error
shortDescription: The washer could not drain the water within the allowed time period.
fullDescription: Error E13 indicates the washer failed to drain water from the tub within the maximum allowed time. The drain pump may be blocked, the drain hose kinked or clogged, or the pump itself may have failed. Standing water will remain in the drum.

causes.cause: Drain pump filter is clogged with debris, coins, or small items
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller is jammed
Drain pump motor has failed

symptoms: Clothes are soaking wet after cycle
Drain pump makes no sound or sounds strained

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access door at the bottom front of the washer. Place towels and a shallow container under it. Slowly turn the filter cap counterclockwise to release trapped water. Remove the filter and clear all debris, coins, hair, and lint.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose from the washer to the standpipe or sink. Ensure it is not kinked, pinched, or pushed too far into the standpipe (maximum 6 inches).

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, look into the pump cavity with a flashlight. Check if the impeller spins freely. Remove any debris wrapped around it.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter and hose are clear but the pump does not run, disconnect power and access the pump from the bottom. Test the pump motor for resistance (typically 5-20 ohms). Replace if it reads OL.

whenToCallPro: If the pump filter is clean and the hose is clear but the pump still does not drain, professional diagnosis of the pump motor or control board is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E13 drain error pump filter fix

faq.q: Where is the drain pump filter on a Bosch washer?
faq.a: It is behind a small access door at the bottom front of the machine. Open the door and you will see a round cap that unscrews counterclockwise.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain pump filter?
faq.a: Bosch recommends cleaning the drain pump filter at least every 1-2 months to prevent buildup of lint, hair, and small items.

faq.q: Can a sock cause E13?
faq.a: Yes, small items like socks, coins, hairpins, and underwire from bras are common causes of drain pump clogs.

title: Fill Timeout Error
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with enough water within the maximum allowed fill time.
fullDescription: Error E17 (also shown as F17 on some models) indicates the washer could not fill to the required water level within the timeout period. This is typically caused by water supply issues, a clogged inlet screen, or a faulty water inlet valve.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets are not fully open
Inlet hose filter screens are clogged
Water inlet valve is defective
Water pressure is too low

symptoms: E17 or F17 error on display
No water or very little water in the drum
Washer stops early in the cycle
No water flow sound during fill

fixSteps.title: Check water supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Verify that both hot and cold water supply valves behind the washer are fully open. Turn them counterclockwise to the fully open position.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet hose filter screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply. Disconnect both hoses from the back of the washer. Remove the small mesh filter screens from inside the valve inlets and from the hose ends. Clean off any sediment or debris.

fixSteps.title: Test water inlet valves
fixSteps.instruction: With a multimeter, test each solenoid coil on the water inlet valve. Normal resistance is 500-1500 ohms per coil. Replace the valve if any coil reads OL or near zero.

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Bosch washers need at least 20 PSI water pressure. If other fixtures have low flow, the household pressure may be insufficient. A pressure gauge on the supply line can verify.

whenToCallPro: If water supply and valves are fine but the washer still does not fill, the control board's valve output or pressure sensor may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E17 F17 fill error water inlet fix

faq.q: Is E17 the same as F17?
faq.a: Yes, some Bosch washer models display this as E17 and others as F17. They indicate the same fill timeout condition.

faq.q: Can frozen pipes cause E17?
faq.a: Yes. In winter, pipes in exterior walls or unheated spaces can freeze, cutting off water supply to the washer.

faq.q: Why does E17 happen with only cold or only hot water?
faq.a: If only one supply is restricted, the washer may not detect enough total flow. Check both hot and cold supply lines.

title: Drain Pump Error
shortDescription: The drain pump is not operating or cannot remove water from the tub.
fullDescription: Error E18 (also F18 on some models) indicates the washer's drain pump cannot evacuate water. Unlike E13 which is a drain timeout, E18 specifically points to the drain pump not operating properly. The pump may be electrically or mechanically failed.

causes.cause: Drain pump is blocked by foreign objects
Drain pump motor has failed
Wiring to the drain pump is damaged
Control board output for drain pump has failed

symptoms: E18 or F18 error on display
No drain pump sound during drain cycle
Washer will not advance to spin cycle

fixSteps.title: Clean the pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the pump filter access door at the bottom front. Place towels and a container. Slowly open the filter cap to drain water, then fully remove and clean the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter out, look into the pump housing. Check that the impeller spins freely. Remove any debris wrapped around it.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump from below or rear. Disconnect the wiring and test resistance (typically 5-20 ohms for a good pump motor). Also check for voltage at the pump connector during a drain cycle.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump is faulty, remove the hose connections and mounting screws. Install the new pump, reconnect hoses and wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the pump tests fine electrically but gets no power from the control board, professional diagnosis is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E18 F18 drain pump error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E13 and E18?
faq.a: E13 is a drain timeout (water did not drain in time) while E18 specifically indicates the drain pump is not operating. E13 can be caused by blockages; E18 often means pump failure.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the washer?
faq.a: Yes. Use the emergency drain hose (small hose next to the pump filter) or slowly open the pump filter cap to drain water into a shallow container.

faq.q: Is E18 the same as F18?
faq.a: Yes, some models display E18 and others F18 for the same drain pump fault.

title: AquaStop Leak Detection Activated
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base pan, activating the AquaStop anti-flood system.
fullDescription: Error E23 indicates the AquaStop float switch in the base pan has been triggered by accumulated water. This is a flood protection feature. The washer will stop all operations and attempt to drain. The leak source must be found and repaired, and the base pan dried, before the washer will operate again.

causes.cause: Detergent dispenser hose is leaking
Door seal/gasket (bellows) is torn or damaged
Tub-to-pump hose connection is loose
Excessive suds causing overflow through vents

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power and water
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and shut off both water supply valves immediately.

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the washer back to drain water from the base pan into towels. Alternatively, remove the bottom access panel and soak up water.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door seal for tears, the dispenser hose connections, and all tub-to-pump hoses for looseness or cracks. Run a short cycle with the bottom panel removed to visually locate the leak.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose hose clamps, replace a damaged door seal, or replace a cracked hose as needed. Ensure the base pan is completely dry before testing.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot locate the leak or if the tub itself is cracked, professional repair is necessary.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E23 AquaStop leak error fix

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause E23?
faq.a: Yes. Excessive suds can overflow through tub vents into the base pan, triggering the AquaStop. Always use HE (high-efficiency) detergent in the recommended amount.

faq.q: How do I reset E23?
faq.a: You must first dry the base pan completely so the float switch drops back down. Then unplug the washer for 5 minutes and reconnect. The error should clear.

faq.q: Will E23 keep coming back?
faq.a: Yes, unless you find and fix the leak source. The AquaStop will trigger every time water accumulates in the base pan.

title: Pressure Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is providing erratic or out-of-range readings.
fullDescription: Error E26 indicates the analog pressure sensor (or pressure switch) that monitors water level in the tub is malfunctioning. The control board is receiving signals that do not correspond to expected water levels, preventing proper fill and drain control.

causes.cause: Pressure sensor has failed
Air trap or pressure chamber is blocked
Pressure hose is cracked or disconnected
Control board analog input is faulty

symptoms: Water level seems inconsistent between cycles
May alternate with other fill/drain errors

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the rubber hose running from the tub to the pressure sensor (usually on the top or rear of the machine). Check for cracks, kinks, or disconnections.

fixSteps.title: Clean the pressure chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure hose from the tub connection. Clean out any debris, lint, or scale from the pressure chamber opening.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure sensor. Gently blow into the hose connection and listen for a click (mechanical switch) or measure voltage change (analog sensor) with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor from its mount, disconnect the hose and wiring. Install the new sensor and reconnect everything.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and hose are fine, the control board's pressure input circuit may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E26 pressure sensor error fix

faq.q: Can fabric softener clog the pressure chamber?
faq.a: Yes, fabric softener residue can accumulate in the pressure chamber over time. Regular cleaning helps prevent this.

faq.q: Will E26 cause flooding?
faq.a: Potentially. If the sensor reads too low, the washer may overfill. The AquaStop system provides a secondary safety measure.

faq.q: How does the pressure sensor work?
faq.a: As water rises in the tub, it compresses air in a sealed chamber. The pressure sensor measures this air pressure to determine water level.

title: Water Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow meter is not detecting proper water flow during fill operations.
fullDescription: Error E29 indicates the flow sensor in the inlet system is not registering water flow as expected. The control board uses this sensor to measure exact water volume entering the tub. A faulty sensor, debris in the flow meter, or very low water pressure can trigger this error.

causes.cause: Flow meter impeller is stuck or clogged
Flow sensor has failed electronically
Very low incoming water pressure
Wiring to the flow sensor is damaged

symptoms: Washer does not fill or overfills
Incorrect water amount for selected cycle
May see E17 error alternating with E29

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both hot and cold supply valves are fully open. Test flow from nearby fixtures to confirm adequate pressure.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off water supply. Remove the inlet hoses and clean the filter screens at the valve inlets. Scale and sediment can restrict flow enough to affect the flow meter.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flow meter in the inlet assembly. Remove it and check that the internal impeller spins freely. Clean any debris or mineral deposits.

fixSteps.title: Replace the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, replace the flow meter. Disconnect wiring and plumbing connections, remove the old unit, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the flow meter is clean and functional but E29 persists, a professional can test the control board's flow meter input.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer E29 flow sensor error fix

faq.q: What does the flow meter do?
faq.a: The flow meter measures the exact volume of water entering the washer so the control board can fill to the precise level needed for each cycle.

faq.q: Can hard water cause E29?
faq.a: Yes. Mineral deposits from hard water can coat the flow meter impeller, slowing it or stopping it. Regular descaling helps prevent this.

faq.q: Is E29 serious?
faq.a: It prevents proper washing but is not dangerous. The washer may overfill or underfill. Address it to ensure proper water levels and clothing care.

title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The door lock mechanism has failed to engage or the control board cannot confirm the door is locked.
fullDescription: Error F16 indicates the door lock assembly cannot secure the door or the lock switch is not sending the correct feedback signal. The washer requires a confirmed locked door before filling with water. This error prevents the washer from starting any cycle.

causes.cause: Door lock assembly is worn or broken
Door lock switch is defective
Door latch or striker is misaligned
Wiring to the door lock is damaged

symptoms: Door does not click locked when closed
Door can be opened even when washer should be locked

fixSteps.title: Check door closure
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is caught in the door seal preventing full closure. Check that the door striker aligns properly with the lock mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the door lock by removing the front panel or door boot retaining spring. Test the lock switch with a multimeter for continuity when activated.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring harness from the door lock to the control board. Look for pinched or broken wires, especially where the harness flexes through the door opening.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the door boot retaining spring, peel back the seal, unscrew the lock from the front panel, disconnect the connector, and install the new lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the lock assembly and wiring are fine, the control board may not be sending the lock signal, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F16 door lock error fix

faq.q: Why is the door lock important?
faq.a: The door lock prevents the door from opening during a cycle when the drum contains water, preventing flooding and potential injury from a spinning drum.

faq.q: Can I open the door manually if F16 occurs?
faq.a: If the door is stuck locked, disconnect power and wait several minutes. Most Bosch washers have an emergency door release accessible from behind the pump filter door.

faq.q: How long does a door lock assembly last?
faq.a: Typically 7-10 years. Frequent use and forceful door closing can accelerate wear.

title: Water Inlet Timeout
shortDescription: Water is not entering the washer within the expected time period.
fullDescription: Error F17 (also displayed as E17 on some models) means the washer has not received enough water within the maximum fill time. This is essentially the same error as E17 and indicates a water supply or inlet valve issue.

causes.cause: Water supply valves are closed or partially closed
Inlet filter screens are clogged with sediment
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Household water pressure is below 20 PSI

symptoms: Washer does not advance past the fill stage
Cycle cancels after a few minutes

fixSteps.title: Open supply valves fully
fixSteps.instruction: Check the hot and cold supply valves behind the washer. Turn both fully counterclockwise to ensure maximum water flow.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off water. Disconnect the inlet hoses and clean the small mesh filter screens in the valve inlets. Reconnect and restore water.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across each inlet valve coil with a multimeter. Each coil should read 500-1500 ohms. An OL reading indicates a failed coil.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve if needed
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect water supply, remove hoses, disconnect wiring, and remove the valve mounting screws. Install the new valve and reconnect everything.

whenToCallPro: If the supply and valve are fine, the pressure sensor or control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F17 water inlet timeout fix

faq.q: Is F17 the same as E17?
faq.a: Yes, they are the same error. Different Bosch model generations display the code differently.

faq.q: Can I use the washer if F17 only happens sometimes?
faq.a: Intermittent F17 usually means partially clogged inlet screens or marginal water pressure. Clean the screens and check pressure to prevent it from becoming permanent.

faq.q: Do both hot and cold need to be connected?
faq.a: Yes. Bosch washers use both hot and cold inlets. If one is not connected, certain cycles may not fill properly.

title: Drain Pump Failure
shortDescription: The drain pump has failed to remove water from the tub.
fullDescription: Error F18 (also E18 on some models) indicates the drain pump is not functioning. The control board has attempted to activate the pump but water remains in the tub. This can be a mechanical blockage, pump motor failure, or electrical issue.

causes.cause: Foreign object blocking the pump impeller
Drain pump motor burned out
Drain hose is completely blocked
Control board not sending power to the pump

symptoms: No pump sound during drain cycle

fixSteps.title: Drain manually and clean filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the pump filter door at the bottom front. Use the emergency drain tube or slowly open the filter cap to drain water. Remove and thoroughly clean the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check the impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter removed, inspect the pump cavity. Ensure the impeller turns freely and no objects are lodged inside.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the drain hose is not kinked, crushed, or pushed too far into the standpipe. Maximum insertion is 6 inches.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: If above steps do not resolve, test pump motor resistance (5-20 ohms normal) and replace if failed.

whenToCallPro: If the pump and hose are clear but the pump receives no power, the control board needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F18 drain pump failure fix

faq.q: How do I access the emergency drain?
faq.a: Open the small door at the bottom front of the washer. You will see a small rubber hose with a cap. Pull it out, place in a container, and remove the cap to drain slowly.

faq.q: What commonly blocks the pump filter?
faq.a: Coins, hairpins, buttons, small socks, and lint are the most common items found in the pump filter.

faq.q: Is F18 the same as E18?
faq.a: Yes. Different model generations display the same drain pump error as either F18 or E18.

title: Heating System Error
shortDescription: The washer's water heating system has failed or cannot reach the selected temperature.
fullDescription: Error F19 indicates the washer's heating element is not heating the water to the programmed temperature within the allowed time. This can be caused by a failed heating element, faulty temperature sensor, or control board issue. Cycles requiring hot water will not work properly.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out
NTC temperature sensor is faulty
Control board heating relay has failed
Wiring to the heating element is damaged

symptoms: Water is cold during warm or hot cycles
Cycle takes much longer than expected
Clothes not clean on warm wash settings

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes. Reconnect and run a warm cycle. If F19 returns, the heating system needs diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heating element (usually at the bottom of the tub, accessed from the rear panel). Disconnect wiring and measure resistance. Normal is 20-40 ohms. Also check for ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is near the heating element. Disconnect and measure resistance. At room temperature it should be approximately 10-50k ohms depending on the model.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater or sensor is faulty, replace it. The heating element slides into the tub through a rubber grommet sealed with a bolt. Tighten the bolt to compress the grommet and create a seal.

whenToCallPro: If the heater and sensor test fine, the control board relay or wiring needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F19 heating error fix

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water while F19 is active?
faq.a: The cold wash setting may work since it does not use the heater. However, warm and hot settings will not heat the water.

faq.q: Why does the cycle take so long with F19?
faq.a: The washer may attempt to heat the water for an extended period before timing out and showing the error.

faq.q: Is F19 dangerous?
faq.a: F19 itself is not dangerous. It means the heater is not working, not that it is overheating. However, if F19 is caused by a ground fault, it should be repaired promptly.

title: Uncontrolled Water Heating
shortDescription: Water temperature is rising higher than expected, indicating uncontrolled heating.
fullDescription: Error F20 is a safety alert indicating the water temperature has exceeded the target and is continuing to rise. This means the heating element is not being properly controlled by the control board, or the temperature sensor is reading incorrectly low. The washer immediately shuts down to prevent scalding water and damage.

causes.cause: Control board heating relay is stuck closed
NTC temperature sensor is reading too low
Heater wiring is shorted, bypassing control
Incoming hot water supply temperature is too high

symptoms: Steam or excessive heat from the washer

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer or turn off the circuit breaker immediately if you notice excessive heat or steam. Do not open the door until the water has cooled.

fixSteps.title: Check water heater setting
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your household water heater is set to no more than 120F/49C. Excessively hot incoming water can trigger F20.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access and test the temperature sensor. If it reads much lower resistance than expected at room temperature, it is telling the board the water is cold when it is not.

fixSteps.title: Professional control board diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the NTC sensor is fine, the control board relay is likely stuck. This requires professional diagnosis and likely board replacement.

whenToCallPro: F20 is a safety-critical error. If the sensor tests fine, the control board needs professional replacement. Do not continue using the washer until repaired.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F20 overheating error fix

faq.q: Is F20 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, F20 indicates uncontrolled heating which can cause scalding water and potential damage to clothes and washer components. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: Can I open the door with F20?
faq.a: Wait at least 30 minutes for the water to cool before attempting to open the door. Opening with hot water inside can cause scalding.

faq.q: What causes the heating relay to stick?
faq.a: Power surges, age-related wear, or manufacturing defects can cause a relay to weld itself closed, keeping the heater permanently on.

title: Motor Control Module Failure
shortDescription: The motor control module (MCU) has detected an internal fault and cannot operate the motor.
fullDescription: Error F21 indicates the motor control module (also called the motor inverter or motor control unit) has experienced a failure. This sophisticated electronic module converts AC power to the precise signals needed to drive the motor at varying speeds. Internal component failure prevents motor operation.

causes.cause: Motor control module has internally failed
Power surge damaged the MCU
Motor is drawing excessive current, tripping MCU protection
Overheating of the MCU due to poor ventilation

symptoms: Motor does not run at all
Drum does not spin in any cycle
May hear a click when cycle tries to start
Error appears immediately when motor should start

fixSteps.title: Allow cooling
fixSteps.instruction: If the washer has been running multiple cycles, allow it to cool for 2 hours. Overheated MCUs can trigger F21 as a protection. Try again after cooling.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 15 minutes to allow the MCU to fully reset. A glitch may cause a false F21.

fixSteps.title: Check motor
fixSteps.instruction: If F21 persists, check the motor for mechanical issues. A seized bearing or jammed drum puts excessive load on the MCU. Try spinning the drum by hand with the belt removed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the MCU
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is free and the MCU is confirmed faulty, it must be replaced. This involves removing the rear panel, disconnecting the MCU connectors, and installing the new unit.

whenToCallPro: MCU replacement should ideally be done by a professional to ensure proper installation and avoid damage to the expensive replacement part.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F21 motor control module error fix

faq.q: What is the motor control module?
faq.a: The MCU is an electronic board that precisely controls motor speed and direction. It converts household AC power into the variable signals needed for different wash and spin speeds.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F21?
faq.a: Yes. Voltage spikes can damage the sensitive electronics in the MCU. A surge protector is recommended for front-load washers.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the MCU?
faq.a: If the washer is less than 8 years old, MCU replacement ($200-$450) is usually worthwhile. Compare against the cost of a new washer.

title: AquaStop System Error
shortDescription: The AquaStop anti-flood protection system has detected a malfunction in its own circuitry.
fullDescription: Error F23 indicates a fault within the AquaStop system itself rather than a detected leak. The AquaStop valve or its control circuitry is not responding properly. This may be a problem with the AquaStop hose assembly, the magnetic valve, or the wiring.

causes.cause: AquaStop valve (on the supply hose) has failed
Wiring to the AquaStop valve is damaged
AquaStop float switch is stuck
Control board AquaStop circuit has failed

symptoms: Washer will not fill with water
AquaStop hose may feel hot or vibrating
Clicking sounds from the AquaStop valve
Washer stops before any water enters

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and try again. A temporary glitch in the AquaStop circuit may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check AquaStop valve
fixSteps.instruction: The AquaStop valve is the bulky connector on the water supply hose where it attaches to the faucet. Check for visible damage, leaking, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Test AquaStop electrical
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the AquaStop hose assembly. Test the valve coil resistance (typically 2-4k ohms). Also check the wiring from the washer to the AquaStop connector.

fixSteps.title: Replace the AquaStop hose
fixSteps.instruction: If the AquaStop valve has failed, the entire hose assembly must be replaced as it is an integrated unit. Shut off water, disconnect old hose, install new hose.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the AquaStop hose does not clear F23, the washer's internal AquaStop circuitry on the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F23 AquaStop system error fix

faq.q: What is the AquaStop system?
faq.a: AquaStop is Bosch's integrated flood protection system. It includes a special supply hose with a shut-off valve, a base pan with a float switch, and electronic monitoring to detect and prevent leaks.

faq.q: Can I use a regular hose instead of the AquaStop hose?
faq.a: No. The AquaStop hose contains an electronic valve that must communicate with the washer's control board. A standard hose will trigger F23.

faq.q: How long does the AquaStop hose last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years. Replace it if you notice any cracks, leaks, or if F23 appears and the valve tests faulty.

title: Door Lock Mechanism Failure
shortDescription: The door lock cannot engage or disengage properly.
fullDescription: Error F34 is a more specific door lock error than F16. It indicates the lock mechanism is physically stuck or jammed, either in the locked or unlocked position. The lock motor or solenoid within the lock assembly has failed.

causes.cause: Door lock solenoid or motor has burned out
Lock mechanism is jammed with detergent residue
Door striker is bent or out of alignment
Lock assembly wiring is broken

symptoms: Door physically locked and cannot be opened
Door will not lock when closing
Clicking or buzzing from the lock area

fixSteps.title: Wait and try
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and wait 5 minutes. The lock may release when power is removed. Try gently opening the door.

fixSteps.title: Use emergency release
fixSteps.instruction: If the door is stuck locked, access the emergency door release. On Bosch washers, pull the small handle or cord behind the pump filter access door.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Peel back the door boot seal to access the lock. Check for visible damage, jammed parts, or detergent buildup preventing movement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock mechanism is failed, remove it by disconnecting the wiring and removing mounting screws. Install the new lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot release the door or if the lock and wiring appear fine, the control board may have a fault.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F34 door lock stuck error fix

faq.q: My door is stuck locked with F34. How do I get my clothes out?
faq.a: Disconnect power and wait 5 minutes. If the door does not release, use the emergency release tab behind the pump filter door at the bottom front of the washer.

faq.q: Can a power outage cause F34?
faq.a: Yes. If power is lost during a cycle, the lock may remain engaged. After power is restored, try running a short cycle or use the emergency release.

faq.q: Is F34 the same as F16?
faq.a: Both relate to the door lock, but F16 is typically a lock feedback signal issue while F34 indicates the lock mechanism itself is stuck or jammed.

title: Motor Not Turning - Drum Seized
shortDescription: The motor cannot rotate the drum due to a mechanical blockage or seized bearing.
fullDescription: Error F43 indicates the motor is receiving power but the drum is not rotating. The control board detects the motor drawing current without corresponding tachometer feedback. This typically means the drum is mechanically blocked or the bearings have seized.

causes.cause: Foreign object jammed between the drum and tub
Drum bearings have seized
Drive belt is broken or off the pulleys
Brake mechanism is stuck engaged

symptoms: Drum will not rotate at all
Motor buzzes or hums without drum movement
Drum is very hard to turn by hand
Grinding or scraping sounds before error appeared

fixSteps.title: Check for drum blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and try rotating the drum by hand. It should turn freely. If it is hard to turn, something may be jammed between the drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel. Check that the drive belt is on the motor and drum pulleys and is not broken or frayed. A loose or snapped belt will not turn the drum.

fixSteps.title: Look for foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: With the rear panel off, look between the outer tub and the drum. Items like coins, underwire, or small garments can slip through and jam the drum. Use a flashlight to inspect.

fixSteps.title: Check drum bearings
fixSteps.instruction: If the drum turns but feels gritty, grinding, or rough, the bearings are failing. Try lifting the drum from inside the front opening. If there is significant play (more than 1/4 inch), the bearings are worn.

whenToCallPro: Drum bearing replacement requires complete tub disassembly and is a major repair best handled by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F43 drum not turning error fix

faq.q: How do I know if my drum bearings are bad?
faq.a: Spin the drum by hand with the washer off. If it feels gritty, makes grinding sounds, or if there is noticeable play when you push the drum up and down, the bearings are likely worn.

faq.q: Is drum bearing replacement worth it?
faq.a: Bearing replacement is a major job ($300-$500 professionally). If the washer is more than 8 years old, a new washer may be more cost-effective.

faq.q: Can a broken belt cause F43?
faq.a: Yes. A broken belt means the motor turns but the drum does not. The tachometer may still report motor speed, but some models detect the load difference.

title: Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (NTC) is sending out-of-range readings.
fullDescription: Error F57 indicates the NTC temperature sensor used to monitor wash water temperature is providing readings outside the expected range. This prevents the control board from properly regulating heating. The sensor may be open, shorted, or reading inaccurately.

causes.cause: NTC temperature sensor has failed
Sensor connector has corroded or come loose
Wiring to the sensor is damaged
Control board sensor input is faulty

symptoms: Cycle may stop during heating phase
Works on cold settings but not warm or hot

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the NTC sensor, usually near the heating element at the bottom of the tub. Check that the connector is secure and free of corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At room temperature (25C/77F), it should read approximately 10-50k ohms depending on the model. OL or near-zero readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests within range, check the wiring from the sensor to the control board for breaks or damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the old sensor out of its housing in the tub. Push the new sensor in until it seats. Reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test fine, the control board may have a faulty input circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F57 temperature sensor NTC error fix

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: NTC stands for Negative Temperature Coefficient. It is a type of resistor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases, allowing the control board to measure water temperature.

faq.q: Can F57 cause the washer to overheat?
faq.a: If the sensor reads too low (indicating cold when water is actually hot), the heater may run excessively. However, the F20 safety protection should activate before dangerous temperatures are reached.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor the same part as in a Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: Sometimes the same part number is used across Bosch appliances. Check the specific part number for your washer model.

title: Main Control Board Software Error
shortDescription: The main control board has encountered an unrecoverable software or firmware error.
fullDescription: Error F63 indicates a critical software or firmware error in the main control board. The processor has encountered a state from which it cannot recover. This is often caused by power fluctuations, corrupted memory, or age-related hardware degradation on the board.

causes.cause: Power surge or interruption corrupted board memory
Control board firmware corruption
Control board component failure
Moisture damage to the control board

fixSteps.title: Extended power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for at least 30 minutes. This allows all capacitors to discharge and the processor to fully cold-start. Plug back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and inspect the control board for signs of moisture, condensation, or corrosion. A leak from the dispenser area is a common cause of board damage.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outlet provides stable 120V AC. Unstable power can cause repeated software errors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If F63 persists after multiple resets, the control board needs replacement. Remove the top panel, disconnect all connectors, swap in the new board.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement is recommended as a professional repair to ensure correct installation and avoid damaging the new board.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch washer F63 control board error fix

faq.q: Can F63 be fixed without replacing the board?
faq.a: Sometimes a thorough power reset (30+ minutes unplugged) clears F63. If it returns, the board likely needs replacement or professional reprogramming.

faq.q: How much does a Bosch washer control board cost?
faq.a: Typically $150-$350 for the part. Professional installation adds $100-$200 in labor.

faq.q: How can I prevent F63?
faq.a: Use a surge protector on the washer circuit. Ensure the dispenser area does not leak onto the control board area. Avoid interrupting power during cycles.
